Ukraine Conflict News





21 December 2025 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • Venezuela slams US 'theft, hijacking' of second oil tanker, vows to take case to UN Press TV 21 Dec 2025 -- The Venezuelan government has categorically denounced the United States' "theft and hijacking" of a second oil tanker off the country's coast, vowing to pursue legal avenues against Washington, including filing a complaint with the UN.
  • Araghchi: US actions against Venezuela imperil regional and global peace IRNA 21 Dec 2025 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has condemned US threats to use force against Venezuela, calling them a blatant violation of the fundamental principles of international law and the United Nations Charter and a threat to world peace.
  • US actions against Venezuela endanger global, regional peace: Araghchi Press TV 21 Dec 2025 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has expressed Iran's solidarity with the Venezuelan people and government amid the United States' "illegal" maritime attacks and threats to use force against the South American country, saying Washington's hostile conducts endanger world peace.
  • US intercepts another tanker off Venezuela as Washington escalates pressure campaign against Caracas Press TV 21 Dec 2025 -- US forces have stopped another oil tanker off the coast of Venezuela, marking the second such operation in less than two weeks as President Donald Trump presses ahead with a hardline campaign aimed at tightening pressure on President Nicolas Maduro's government.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Denys Shmyhal holds talks on security cooperation with Portugal's Minister of National Defence Ukraine MOD 21 Dec 2025
  • During a working trip to Zaporizhzhia, Minister of Defence Denys Shmyhal visited an air defense center and combat units Ukraine MOD 21 Dec 2025

  • M109A7s to improve mobility, precision, firepower of Taiwan's artillery: Expert CNA 21 Dec 2025 -- The upcoming additions of U.S.-made M109A7 howitzers will boost Taiwan's artillery with higher mobility, better precision and more firepower, a local defense expert told CNA on Sunday.

  • Iranian naval flotillas set sail for South Africa, Navy commander says IRNA 21 Dec 2025 -- The Commander of the Iranian Army's Naval Force, Rear Admiral Shahram Irani, says the 103rd and 104th flotillas of the Iranian Naval Force have set sail for South Africa.
  • Iran Navy dispatches task groups to South Africa as it expands maritime presence: Cmdr. Press TV 21 Dec 2025 -- Iran's Navy commander praised the country's "expanding maritime presence" beyond the region, announcing that the Navy's 103rd and 104th task groups have set sail on overseas missions and are currently en route to South Africa.

  • Japan's Nuclear Ambitions 'Must Be Prevented at Any Cost' - North Korea Sputnik 21 Dec 2025 -- A senior Japanese government official recently stated that his country should possess nuclear weapons.

  • The commissioning ceremony of 2nd Pakistan Navy MILGEM class ship PNS KHAIBAR was held at Istanbul Naval Shipyard, Türkiye. ISPR Pakistan 21 Dec 2025 -- The commissioning ceremony of 2nd Pakistan Navy MILGEM class ship PNS KHAIBAR was held at Istanbul Naval Shipyard, Türkiye.

  • Chinese expert warns proliferation risk as South Korea, US plan consultations on nuclear-powered submarines next year Global Times 21 Dec 2025 -- South Korea and the US reportedly plan to conduct consultations on the construction of South Korea's nuclear-powered submarines, uranium enrichment, and reprocessing of spent nuclear fuel next year. A Chinese expert said on Sunday that South Korea and the US' nuclear-powered submarine plan poses serious threats to nuclear non-proliferation, risks an arms race and undermines peace and stability.

  • Estonia to Purchase at Least Six South Korean-Made Chunmoo Multiple Rocket Launchers Estonia CDI 21 Dec 2025 -- The Estonian Centre for Defence Investments (ECDI) and representatives of the South Korean defence industry company Hanwha Aerospace have signed a contract for the procurement of six Chunmoo multiple rocket launchers.

  • PM call with President Trump of the United States: 21 December 2025 Gov.UK 21 Dec 2025

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

    Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • The Chief of the General Staff at a Hanukkah Candle-Lighting With Hashmonaim Brigade Troops: "You are the Maccabees of our time" Israel Defense Forces 21 Dec 2025

  • Israel's hawkish minister announces approval of 19 new settlements in West Bank Press TV 21 Dec 2025 -- Israel's so-called security cabinet has given the final approval to a highly controversial project of 19 new settlements in the occupied West Bank, despite international condemnation and growing calls for a halt to settlement activity.

  • Satellite images indicate Israel entrenching a permanent military presence in Gaza Press TV 21 Dec 2025 -- The Israeli regime appears to be laying the groundwork for a permanent military presence in large parts of the Gaza Strip, according to a new satellite imagery analysis that documents extensive construction, infrastructure expansion, and continued destruction of Palestinian property since a ceasefire took effect in October.

  • OIC Condemns Israeli Occupation's Targeting of a School for Displaced Persons in Gaza City Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 21 Dec 2025 -- The Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) strongly condemned the ongoing crimes committed by the Israeli occupation in the Gaza Strip, the latest of which was the bombing of a school sheltering displaced persons, resulting in the martyrdom of seven Palestinians and the injury of dozens of innocent civilians, in flagrant violation of international humanitarian law

    • Other Conflicts

  • Yemeni Gov't, Houthis Make Progress in Prisoner Swap Talks - Source Sputnik 21 Dec 2025 -- The internationally recognized Yemeni government and the rebel Ansar Allah movement (also known as the Houthis) have made progress in prisoner exchange negotiations taking place in the Omani capital of Muscat under the auspices of the United Nations, a Yemeni government source told Sputnik.

  • On 19 December 2025, nine khwarij belonging to Indian Proxy Fitna Al Khwarij were killed in two separate engagements in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Province. ISPR Pakistan 21 Dec 2025

  • Ongoing Hostilities Between Cambodia and Thailand US Dept. of State 21 Dec 2025

News Reports

  • Putin Ready to Engage in Dialogue With Macron - Kremlin Sputnik 21 Dec 2025 -- Russian President Vladimir Putin is ready to engage in dialogue with French President Emmanuel Macron, and if there is mutual political will, then the French leader's intentions can only be assessed positively, Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov told Sputnik.

  • China's largest offshore oil production base, Bohai Oilfield, sees 2025 output exceed 40 million tons Global Times 21 Dec 2025 -- China National Offshore Oil Corporation (CNOOC) on Sunday announced cumulative output from the offshore oil and gas at the Bohai Oilfield, China's largest crude oil production base, has exceeded 40 million tons, setting a new record and providing solid support for national energy security and high-quality economic and social development.

  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 21 Dec 2025

  • Press Statement by Director of Institute for Japan Studies under DPRK Foreign Ministry KCNA 21 Dec 2025 -- On December 20, the director of the Institute for Japan Studies under the DPRK Foreign Ministry released the following press statement "The war criminal state Japan's attempt to go nuclear must be prevented at any cost as it will bring mankind a great disaster":

  • Israeli threats signal disarray as Iran closely monitors developments: Cmdr. IRNA 21 Dec 2025 -- The deputy chief of General Staff of the Armed Forces of the Islamic Republic of Iran has said that repeated threats by the Israeli regime are a sign of turmoil and weakness, stressing that Iran is closely monitoring all regional and international developments.
  • Israel using threats, media hype to hide blows suffered in Iran war: Top commander Press TV 21 Dec 2025 -- A senior Iranian military commander says Israel is resorting to threats, political maneuvers, and media campaigns to cover up the severe defeat that the regime suffered in its illegal aggression against the Islamic Republic earlier this year.

  • Pashinian Vs. The Catholicos: What's Behind The Armenian Prime Minister's Campaign Against The Church? RFE/RL 21 Dec 2025 -- Armenia is facing its gravest church-state clash since gaining independence, as tensions continue to grow between Prime Minister Nikol Pashinian and Catholicos Garegin II, the supreme head of the Armenian Apostolic Church.
